panels.description: Currently executing queries with their query_id, backend PID, and SQL text. Use this to identify which queries to drill into via the $query_id variable.
panels.title: Active Queries

panels.description: Current elapsed execution time for each query's root node. Use for at-a-glance triage of which queries have been running longest.
panels.title: Query Elapsed Time

panels.description: Count of plan nodes currently spilling to temp files (work_mem exceeded). Any nonzero value means at least one sort or hash is using disk.
panels.title: Spill Alert

panels.description: Interactive plan tree explorer. Nodes colored by time share (red=hot, green=cold). Displays wall-clock time and tuples produced. Click any node to inspect all 14 per-node metrics.
panels.title: Plan Tree

panels.description: All 14 per-node metrics across all active queries. Sort by any column to find problem nodes.
panels.title: Active Query Metrics

panels.description: Wall-clock execution time per plan node. Nodes climbing fastest are actively consuming CPU or waiting on I/O right now.
panels.title: Node Execution Time

panels.description: Each node's percentage share of total query wall-clock time. The highest line is the current bottleneck node.
panels.title: Time Percent by Node

panels.description: Fraction of a parent node's time consumed by each child. The child closest to 1.0 is the critical path through that subtree.
panels.title: Child-to-Parent Time Ratio

panels.description: Cumulative tuples returned by each plan node. Compare sibling nodes to identify data volume asymmetries in joins.
panels.title: Rows Produced by Node

panels.description: Rows a child produces vs rows its parent outputs. Values >>1 indicate the parent filters heavily or the child scans far more data than needed.
panels.title: Row Fan-Out: Child vs Parent

panels.description: Rows discarded by expression filters (WHERE/HAVING) and join conditions per node. High values indicate scan-heavy operations that could benefit from indexing.
panels.title: Rows Filtered

panels.description: Fraction of rows examined then discarded by expression filters. Values above 0.9 mean >90% of scanned rows are thrown away -- missing index or bad filter pushdown.
panels.title: Filter Waste Ratio

panels.description: For join nodes, fraction of processed rows discarded by the join condition. High values (>0.9) = highly selective join. Low values = near-cartesian product.
panels.title: Join Selectivity

panels.description: Loop count of inner-side nodes driven by NestLoop parents. The value equals how many times the inner plan executes, directly multiplying its cost.
panels.title: Nested Loop Amplification

panels.description: Wall-clock time divided by loop count per node. Reveals per-iteration cost independent of how many times a node rescans. Use with Nested Loop Amplification to decide if the problem is per-loop cost or loop count.
panels.title: Per-Loop Node Cost

panels.description: Shows only nodes executed more than once, indicating rescan inside nested loops. The value equals the outer row count driving the inner side.
panels.title: Loops Executed (> 1)

panels.description: Root node startup_time_us (time to first result tuple) vs total_time_us (time to last tuple). The gap shows how long the result set takes to stream out.
panels.title: Tuple Pipeline Latency

panels.description: Total shared buffer hits and misses across all nodes. A growing miss count means the query is reading pages not in shared_buffers.
panels.title: Shared Buffer Activity

panels.description: Shared buffer cache hit ratio across all nodes. Values below 90% for analytic queries indicate the working set exceeds shared_buffers.
panels.title: Cache Hit Ratio

panels.description: Local/temp buffer hits and misses. Relevant for queries using temporary tables or materialized CTEs.
panels.title: Local Buffer Activity

panels.description: Cache misses aggregated by executor node type. Identifies whether SeqScans, IndexScans, or other operations cause the most physical I/O.
panels.title: Buffer Misses by Node Type

panels.description: Physical read block count grouped by parent node. Identifies which branch of the plan tree drives the most disk I/O.
panels.title: Cache Misses by Parent Branch

panels.description: Total wall-clock seconds consumed by each executor node type. Shows whether sorts, scans, joins, or aggregations dominate the query.
panels.title: Time by Node Type

panels.description: Temp file read and write block counts per node. Rising writes = ongoing spill to disk. Rising reads = rescan of spilled data.
panels.title: Spill Activity

panels.description: Microseconds before each node returns its first tuple. High values on sort/hash nodes indicate blocking materialization phases.
panels.title: Startup Time by Node

panels.description: Ratio of startup_time to total_time per node. Values near 1.0 indicate blocking nodes (sorts, hashes) that must fully materialize before producing output.
panels.title: Startup Fraction (Blocking Indicator)

panels.description: 10 nodes with highest wall-clock time across all active queries, joined with query text for cross-query comparison.
panels.title: Top 10 Slowest Nodes
